title: Ubiquitination-anchored signature defines neuroendocrine prostate cancer: hub genes and single-cell ecosystem insights from integrated bioinformatics analysis of public transcriptomic datasets.
publicationName: The aging male : the official journal of the International Society for the Study of the Aging Male
journalName: The aging male : the official journal of the International Society for the Study of the Aging Male
snippet: Neuroendocrine prostate cancer (NEPC) is an aggressive, treatment-refractory state that often emerges under androgen-receptor pathway inhibition. We hypothesized that dysregulated ubiquitination underpins NEPC lineage plasticity and that integrating bulk and single-cell transcriptomes would define a ubiquitination-centered signature and tumor microenvironme...

title: Neoplastic CD3⁺ B cells remodel the DLBCL tumor microenvironment via single-cell and spatial transcriptomics.
snippet: This study constructs a high-resolution multi-omics map of Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma (DLBCL) by integrating single-cell, single-nucleus, and spatial transcriptomics. We identified a previously unrecognized, recurrent subset of malignant B cells that unexpectedly express CD3, a protein typically found only on T cells. This unusual CD3⁺ B cell popu...
